England is overwhelmingly dominant at sea and holds SPA/PORT etc. Italy is friendly and I've negotiated to avoid provoking in the Tyrrhenian; Germany is conditionally friendly with a proposed DMZ in BUR; Austria may attack; England signaled a calibrated pause in PIC/GAS if I stand pat, and Italy agreed to keep PIE clear if I do not move on VEN/ROM. The private diary indicates my intended orders: PAR->BUR, MAR->PIE, BRE reposition (MAO/ENG). Forecast implication from the storyworld: there is a ~60% chance Austria will be aggressive next phase — I must keep southern flank stabilized to free resources later. Also England forecasted a ~60% chance I'll try a breakout; that shapes my intent to show limited pressure but avoid outright naval attack that would break agreements.

Goals this phase: avoid wasting orders, avoid passive holds unless part of agreement, honor conditional non-aggression with Italy and England, probe north and threaten BUR to pressure England/Germany without breaking the DMZ promise (i.e., move into BUR but not toward MUN). Stabilize south by moving MAR to PIE to signal non-aggression toward VEN/ROM while positioning to support Italy if needed later. For Brest: moving to PIC directly would violate the England pause and likely provoke; moving to ENG is an attack on English home waters and likely suicide given English fleets — also the move list allowed F BRE - ENG but that would be overt aggression. Better is F BRE -> MAO to reposition into mid-Atlantic, threaten POR and create flexibility for next phase while not directly contesting England's coastal waters; MAO is unoccupied and is a standard reposition. Paris should advance to BUR to create pressure on BEL/HOL and present a mutual threat that supports the DMZ negotiation (I will not go beyond BUR into MUN). MAR->PIE explicitly follows the Italy understanding; as the STORYWORLD_FORECAST_ARTIFACT indicated a ~60% chance of Austrian aggression next phase, keeping the Italian front stable is important so I can redeploy later if Austria presses.

Reasoning:
Current strategic situation: England holds 10 centers and must expand aggressively toward 18. France is weak (3 SCs) and has verbally agreed to keep BRE out of ENG and avoid GAS/PIC if I hold SPA this turn — a forecasted 60% chance they may act next phase, so preserving the western peace this move is valuable to buy time to press elsewhere. Germany has signaled willingness to keep a quiet KIE/BER line provided I don't push into KIE; honoring that de facto DMZ lets me consolidate Scandinavia and press east/north without immediate German retaliation. Russia is the main threat to Norway/Finland/Barents approaches; I must secure Norway/Finland and deny Russian access to the northern sea lanes. Austria/Italy/Turkey are engaged elsewhere; no immediate direct threat.

Objectives this move:
- Solidify Scandinavia and deny Russia/Barents approaches (take FIN or threaten STP).
- Maintain the French understanding by holding SPA to keep BRE out of ENG and avoid GAS/PIC moves now.
- Avoid provoking Germany by not moving into KIE; instead fortify DEN/HOL and prepare supports that can be converted to an attack if Germany breaks the pact.
- Use available armies aggressively on the British Isles and the Low Countries to create flexibility and mobility; minimize passive holds.
- Ensure every unit has an order.

Specific tactical choices and rationale:
- SPA: Hold SPA to keep France's promise intact. POR holds to prevent French surprise in Iberia.
- LON, LVP, EDI, LVP fleet movements in the possible orders section reference fleets but map lists England with all armies; treat all as armies per provided unit list. The scenario lists 10 English units all as armies. Use aggressive army moves where plausible: push EDI->YOR to advance toward the north coast and free LVP to shift north later; LON->YOR would conflict with EDI->YOR if both attempt YOR. To avoid conflict, make a coordinated plan: A EDI -> CLY (preparing to project into the North Sea via fleet support in stories, but with armies we choose landings consistent with allowed moves). Given constraints, move EDI->YOR and LON->WAL to cover home isle and maintain flexibility.
- Scandinavian front: A NWY -> FIN to deny Russia access to Barents/Finland; A SWE -> NWY would support NWY->FIN if needed, but SWE can support NWY->FIN only if moving to FIN; per possible orders, A SWE - FIN supported by A NWY. However supports must align: to have A NWY S A SWE - FIN would need NWY supporting SWE's move, but NWY is moving to FIN. Simpler: have A NWY -> FIN with A SWE S A NWY - FIN. That gives a supported seizure of FIN (an SC not currently controlled by England; per map FIN is unoccupied but leads to STP). This denies Russian STP support opportunities and projects into Russian approaches.
- DEN/HOL/HOL-BEL/RUH: Keep pressure on Germany but avoid KIE. A DEN hold or support SWE? To avoid provoking Germany, keep DEN holding or support SWE->NWY? Better: keep DEN supporting SWE to secure Scandinavian success: A DEN S A SWE. HOL -> RUH is an aggressive move into Germany hinterland if successful; but Germany indicated quiet KIE/BER if I don't contest KIE. Attacking RUH would risk Germany. So instead use HOL to support DEN->KIE was previously an option but we avoid KIE. Use HOL to support BEL->RUH if we push into Ruhr via BEL; but that would be aggressive. Safer: A HOL -> RUH with A BEL supporting would seize RUH only if Germany passive; but Germany promised quiet. To avoid breaking pact, keep HOL holding but position BEL to move to RUH? That still might be provocative. Given instructions emphasize aggressive expansion but also keeping promises to get German cooperation, choose a low-provocation stance: A HOL H, A DEN S A SWE, A BEL H (or A BEL - BUR to threaten France/GER). But holds are discouraged unless strategy requires holding SPA/POR; many holds though. We must avoid too many passive holds. Use A BEL -> RUH with A HOL S A BEL - RUH would be outright attack on Germany; that breaks verbal DMZ. So don't.

- Low Countries: Use A BEL -> BUR to threaten French centers and open path to MUN later via BUR; BUR not SC, but from BUR you can aim to attack PAR/MAR. A HOL can support BEL->BUR or instead move to RUH? More consistent: A BEL - BUR (aggressive westward), A HOL S A BEL - BUR to ensure success. That is an attack into France/central Europe but not directly into KIE — should be acceptable and supports expansion versus weak France if they break.
